purpose.question: Which of the 23 anchor-eligible typed-core failures already contain a correctly directed context-matched spectral change under the calibrated replicated-anchor instrument?
purpose.calibration_dependency: The parent calibration passed every gate and reproduced 12/12 previously accepted typed-core candidates. This run keeps its seeds, feature extractor, scaler, thresholds, carrier slots, occurrence boundaries, and aggregation unchanged.
purpose.scientific_boundary: This is a complementary automatic remeasurement of existing candidate audio. A pass queues unseen confirmation and blind human QC; it does not rewrite Praat, prove a precise formant transition, or enable product support.

ineligible_failure_cell.reason: Only three of four occurrences passed the immutable context-matched natural-anchor gate in calibration; no candidate evaluation or replacement is allowed.

anchor_reproduction.rule: Rerender all 36 calibrated core cells so voice-global scaling is identical to calibration. Every source/target PCM receipt and all 864 training feature receipts must exactly match the parent calibration before any failed candidate is classified.
anchor_reproduction.mismatch_effect: Fail the run without candidate classification; do not replace a seed, slot, or cell.

candidate_hierarchy.selection_rule: Use the latest already-rendered bounded intervention available for each eligible failure. Twenty-one cells use their immutable v8 lens. The two cells that reached the adaptive grid reuse all six existing WAVs in the original frozen strength order.
candidate_hierarchy.adaptive_occurrence_selection: For each occurrence, choose the first exact-category candidate in frozen order; if none, choose the first directional-only candidate; otherwise leave unresolved. Recompose non-overlapping occurrence windows deterministically and remeasure the composite.
candidate_hierarchy.adaptive_composite_integrity: Require equal finite unclipped PCM, exact neutral outside occurrence windows, frozen boundary metrics, localization, and unchanged selected occurrence classifications.

classification_and_aggregation.instrument: Unchanged bilingual-vowel-spectral-category-v1 features and unchanged context-matched replicated-anchor calibration endpoints.
classification_and_aggregation.occurrence: The natural-anchor gate must already pass. Directional requires cosine >= 0.5, movement >= 25% of the context-matched anchor span, target-distance gain, and source-distance departure. Exact additionally requires movement >= 50% and crossing the endpoint bisector.
classification_and_aggregation.cell: All four occurrences across all three frozen contexts must be directional for directional-only or exact for exact-category. One failure fails the cell.
classification_and_aggregation.nasal: A spectral pass for a nasal cell remains ineligible for blind product QC until a separate nasality-preservation gate passes.
classification_and_aggregation.oral_queue: An oral exact or directional pass enters an automatic candidate queue for unseen typed-fixture confirmation and blind human QC; it is not product enabled.

claim_limits.frozen_results: Do not rewrite v8, word-context, full-context, adaptive-strength, failed cross-context spectral, or replicated-anchor calibration classifications.
claim_limits.spectral: A pass supports broad context-matched spectral movement relative to independently seeded natural endpoints, not exact F1/F2 realization.
claim_limits.generalization: The three frozen carrier contexts do not establish arbitrary-word or arbitrary-sentence generalization; unseen typed fixtures are mandatory next.
claim_limits.listener: Automatic acoustic evidence does not prove Brazilian-Portuguese or American-English listener perception or naturalness.
claim_limits.product: No cell, voice, route, flag, service, or deployment is enabled by this run.

stopping_rule.instruction: Reproduce anchors, evaluate exactly the 23 eligible failures once, retain only objectively completed adaptive composites, preserve all failures, and stop before unseen rendering or human review.
